findings.title: Priority 5 (Standard Processing) is being silently skipped by the decider, stranding Step 1d (integrity intake) and Step 1m (rewrite intake)

evidence.decider_intake_md_step_1d_location_in_cl: line 64 (Step 1d: Integrity Report) — PROP-037 generalization present
evidence.decider_intake_md_step_1m_location_in_cl: line 311 (Step 1m: Rewrite Proposal Intake) — PROP-041 Phase 2c content present
evidence.decider_md_priority_5_definition_in_clon: line 154-156: 'Priority 5 — Standard Processing → Read decider-intake.md, execute full procedure'

findings.root_cause: H3 (budget-pressure / priority-ordering), NOT H1/H2/H4/H5. Both Step 1d and Step 1m are properly defined inside decider-intake.md, AND decider.md's Priority 5 correctly references the full intake procedure. The dispatcher rule says 'after completing priority work, continue to lower priorities in the same run' — but in practice, after P3 (curmudgeon reviews → 2-6 patches), P3b (BAU triage), P4 (expansion integration → 2 patches), the decider judges the run 'done' and jumps to P5b (m1_sweep) without executing P5. Both 2026-05-16T17-21 and 2026-05-17T01-23 runs show this pattern: priority_5_* keys absent from report; m1_sweep ran but P5 (Standard Processing) did not. The architectural problem is that Step 1d and Step 1m are nominally mandatory-every-run intake steps (PROP-037 explicitly says 'EVERY decider invocation' for Step 1d; PROP-041 Phase 2c amendment-001 budgeted '~5 RWs/run intake' for Step 1m) but they live inside Priority 5 which the dispatcher treats as overflow.

ruled_out_hypotheses.H1_prompt_load_order: RULED OUT — Step 1d and Step 1m both present in decider-intake.md (the clone). The reference file is loaded unconditionally at the P5 hook in decider.md.
ruled_out_hypotheses.H2_step_1m_runs_finds_nothing: RULED OUT — manual simulation of Step 1m.A against RW-001 passes: ls returns the file, status='pending', content_hash matches current data/wins.json[id=010].detail_verdict_text (sha256:2a1892bc...), audit-rewrite.js exits 0 with checks C1-C8 all passed. If Step 1m executed it would push RW-001 to the priority queue.
ruled_out_hypotheses.H4_clean_clone_unset: RULED OUT — decider.md Step 0 prelude initializes CLEAN_CLONE before the dispatcher fires; subsequent priorities use it successfully (Step 0b digest gen, P1b ledger read, P3 digest read). Step 1m would have access to the same variable.
ruled_out_hypotheses.H5_PROP_040_conditional_context_load: RULED OUT — PROP-040 only made decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md conditional (Priority 4 only). decider-intake.md remained unconditional at P1/P2/P5. The conditional-load isn't the cause.

patches.rationale: Promote Step 1d and Step 1m from inside Priority 5 (overflow bucket) to their own dedicated priorities BEFORE Priority 3 (heavy patch work). Same pattern decider.md already uses for Step 1f (Priority 1 → onboarding) and Step 1c (Priority 2 → external reports): pull the step out of decider-intake.md and put it at front-of-run where budget pressure can't squeeze it. This is approximately 25 lines added; ~0 lines removed.
patches.insertion_point: AFTER existing Priority 2 (External Reports) block, BEFORE existing Priority 3 (Pending Curmudgeon Reviews) block. Specifically: between the line '→ Read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md`, execute Step 1c.' and the line '**Priority 3 — Pending Curmudgeon Reviews**'.
patches.new_block_to_insert:
**Priority 2b — Integrity Findings Intake** (Step 1d, PROP-037 — check every run)
```bash
ls ${CLEAN_CLONE}/monitor/integrity/report-*.json 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -1
```
Trigger: Latest integrity report exists. EVERY finding with `tracked_under: null` must be promoted to an ISS, regardless of severity. Build drift, next_id collisions, orphan EXPs — all live here. This step is structurally mandatory per PROP-037 (replaces moderate-only filter that masked findings 2026-05-13 → 2026-05-16).
→ Read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md`, execute Step 1d (sections A-D).

**Priority 2c — Rewrite Proposal Intake** (Step 1m, PROP-041 — check every run)
```bash
ls ${CLEAN_CLONE}/monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-*.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l
```
Trigger: Any RW-NNN.json files exist. Two phases per run: (A) intake pending RWs into the curmudgeon priority queue with class='rewrite-verify'; (B) drain curmudgeon-approved RWs by integrating into wins.json / sections.json, then reset rewrite-attempts.json. Step 1m.D escalates RWs stuck in-curmudgeon-review > 20h via HNOTE.
→ Read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md`, execute Step 1m (sub-steps A–E).
patches.secondary_edit: OPTIONAL but recommended: in the existing 'Priority 5 — Standard Processing' block at line 154, add a parenthetical note: 'Note: Step 1d and Step 1m are now lifted to Priority 2b/2c — Priority 5 covers the residual 1a/1b/1e/1g/1h/1h2/1i intake.' This documents the lift so future readers of Priority 5 don't expect those steps to be reached at the P5 hook.
patches.test_validation_after_apply: Run a decider invocation. Verify the daily report includes priority_2b_integrity_intake and priority_2c_rewrite_intake keys (mirroring existing priority_1_new_wins shape). Verify open-issues.next_id increments past 2028 (or at least one ISS-2028+ created from current 7 untracked findings). Verify priority-queue.json gains 2 items with class='rewrite-verify' (RW-001 and RW-002 pushed).

unblock_now_alternative_if_hotfix_not_ap.description: If the operator wants to unblock RW-001/002 immediately without waiting for the decider-md hotfix to land + a decider run to consume it, an in-session manual push is safe. This bypasses Step 1m.A by executing it by hand from a clean clone.
procedure: 1. From a clean clone (/tmp/edit-clone): git pull --rebase origin main
2. node monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-001.json — verify exit 0 (already confirmed 2026-05-17T05:25Z, checks C1-C8 pass)
4. Edit monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json: push 2 items with class='rewrite-verify', target_id='RW-001'/'RW-002', pushed_by='operator', allocate qid=370 and 371, increment pq.next_id to 372
5. Edit monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-001.json: set status='in-curmudgeon-review', popped_by_queue_id=370, popped_by_queue_id_at=now. Same for RW-002 with qid=371.
6. git commit -m 'Manual Step 1m.A bypass: push RW-001/002 to PQ pending decider-md hotfix per DIRECTIVE-20260517-001'
8. workspace-sync's next hourly cycle propagates to FUSE; main curmudgeon picks up qid=370/371 on its next Opus run.
unblock_now_alternative_if_hotfix_not_ap.is_safe_because: (a) RW.original_content_hash still matches current source surface, (b) audit-rewrite.js passes — same gate Step 1m.A would have run, (c) priority-queue.json operator-writes are explicitly permitted per writers field, (d) RW.status mutation is the same additive-edit that Step 1m.A would perform.
unblock_now_alternative_if_hotfix_not_ap.deprecation_after_hotfix: Once the decider-md P2b/P2c hotfix lands AND a decider run executes them cleanly, this manual procedure is no longer needed.

findings.counter_argument: Could the decider be intentionally batching Step 1m intake across multiple runs and just hasn't gotten to it yet? Rebuttal: (1) RW-001 has been pending ~24h, and Step 1m.D explicitly escalates anything in-curmudgeon-review > 20h, which means 'pending → in-curmudgeon-review' MUST have happened by now under the intended cadence — the pending state at 24h is by-definition a stall. (2) PROP-041 amendment-001 budgeted '~5 RWs/run intake' as expected cadence; two consecutive runs with zero intake against two pending RWs that pass all pre-checks is not a budget feature. (3) The same shape independently applies to Step 1d: PROP-037 explicitly says 'EVERY decider invocation' and 7 untracked findings on the freshest integrity report fall through without creating a single ISS. Two intake steps both at zero, in the same dispatcher position (inside P5), for the same two runs, with budget consumed by P3/P3b/P4 patch work — that is the bug signature, not the cadence. (4) The 2026-05-16T17-21 report explicitly enumerates priority_1 through priority_4 keys + m1_sweep but the priority_5 key is absent entirely, which is reporting evidence that decider's own self-bookkeeping considers Priority 5 'never reached' rather than 'reached but no-op'.
findings.operator_action_requested: Apply the decider.md P2b/P2c hotfix patch above. Recommended in-session: operator opens decider.md in a clean clone, inserts the new block after Priority 2 and before Priority 3, commits with message 'decider-md hotfix per DIRECTIVE-20260517-001: lift Step 1d + Step 1m to Priority 2b/2c (fix silent P5 skip)'. Optionally also apply the unblock-now manual push so RW-001/002 reaches curmudgeon in this cycle rather than next.

previous_followup.note: Directive took the full run budget. No follow-up scan of prior PROP verification this run; flag for next Mode 3 / Mode 4 run to walk the proposals directory and verify PROP lifecycle status.

next_run_recommendations: After operator applies the decider.md hotfix, run a Mode 3 cost-engineering pass to verify: (a) priority_2b_integrity_intake and priority_2c_rewrite_intake appear in the next decider report, (b) open-issues.next_id incremented to 2035+ (7 new integrity-derived ISSs), (c) priority-queue.json has rewrite-verify items for RW-001/002. If any of these don't materialize, the bug is not pure budget-pressure and a deeper investigation is warranted.
